name: इंदिरा गांधी राष्ट्रीय विधवा पेंशन योजना / Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
authority: Ministry of Rural Development (NSAP)
benefit_amount: ₹300/month (age 40–79), ₹500/month (80+); most states add a top-up
eligibility: Widow aged 40–79 years
Belongs to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) household
Not remarried
where_to_apply: NSAP portal (nsap.nic.in) or UMANG app, via Gram Panchayat / Block Development Office
common_rejection_reasons: Name mismatch between Aadhaar and death certificate

name: राष्ट्रीय परिवार लाभ योजना / National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS)
authority: Ministry of Rural Development (NSAP)
benefit_amount: ₹20,000 one-time lump sum
eligibility: Death of the primary breadwinner of a BPL family
Deceased was aged 18–59 at the time of death
Application within the state's prescribed window (usually 1–2 years of death)
where_to_apply: NSAP portal (nsap.nic.in) via Block Development Office / Tehsil

name: आयुष्मान भारत प्रधानमंत्री जन आरोग्य योजना / Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY
authority: National Health Authority, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare
benefit_amount: ₹5,00,000/year family health cover (cashless hospitalisation)
eligibility: Family listed in SECC-2011 deprivation categories (widow-headed households are a priority category D5)
No cap on family size or age
where_to_apply: pmjay.gov.in or nearest empanelled hospital's Ayushman Mitra desk / Common Service Centre

name: प्रधानमंत्री उज्ज्वला योजना / PM Ujjwala Yojana 2.0
authority: Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas
benefit_amount: Free LPG connection + first refill and stove; subsidy ₹300 per cylinder (up to 12/year)
eligibility: Adult woman from a poor household without an existing LPG connection
Covered under SECC / BPL / Antyodaya / PMAY / AAY or a self-declaration of poverty
where_to_apply: pmuy.gov.in or any LPG distributor (Indane/Bharat/HP)

name: प्रधानमंत्री वय वंदना योजना / Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY)
authority: LIC of India (Govt. of India backed)
benefit_amount: Assured pension ~7.4%/year on purchase price, up to ₹9,250/month
eligibility: Age 60 or above
Requires a lump-sum purchase (₹1.5 lakh – ₹15 lakh)
Note: closed for new purchase after 31 Mar 2023 — existing policies continue; shown for awareness/servicing
where_to_apply: LIC branch or licindia.in
common_rejection_reasons: Scheme window closed for fresh purchase

name: ईपीएफओ विधवा पेंशन (ईपीएस-95) / EPFO Widow Pension under EPS-95
authority: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation, Ministry of Labour
benefit_amount: Minimum ₹1,000/month (actual depends on husband's pensionable salary/service)
eligibility: Husband was an EPF/EPS member (formal-sector employment)
Payable from date of death, regardless of husband's age or service length
Continues until remarriage; children's pension paid in addition
where_to_apply: EPFO regional office or unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in (via employer)

name: आम आदमी बीमा योजना / LIC Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana (AABY) family benefit
authority: LIC of India / Ministry of Finance
benefit_amount: ₹30,000 natural death / ₹75,000 accidental death payout to nominee; scholarship for 2 children
eligibility: Deceased was enrolled in AABY (rural landless household / notified occupation)
Nominee (widow) files death claim with certificate
Children in class 9–12 eligible for ₹100/month scholarship
where_to_apply: LIC P&GS unit via the nodal agency that enrolled the member
common_rejection_reasons: Claim filed without nodal agency attestation

name: प्रधानमंत्री जीवन ज्योति बीमा योजना दावा / PMJJBY Death Claim
authority: Department of Financial Services, Ministry of Finance
benefit_amount: ₹2,00,000 one-time payout to nominee
eligibility: Husband held a bank account auto-debited ₹436/year for PMJJBY
Death from any cause is covered
Claim filed by nominee at the bank branch
where_to_apply: Bank branch where the deceased held the PMJJBY-linked account (jansuraksha.gov.in)

name: सुकन्या समृद्धि योजना / Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (for daughters)
authority: Ministry of Finance (National Savings Scheme)
benefit_amount: 8.2%/year tax-free interest on deposits for a girl child (min ₹250/year)
eligibility: Girl child below 10 years
Guardian (mother) opens the account
Max 2 accounts per family
where_to_apply: Any post office or authorised bank branch (india.gov.in/sukanya-samriddhi-yojna)

name: उत्तर प्रदेश निराश्रित महिला पेंशन / UP Destitute Widow Pension
authority: Department of Women & Child Development, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh
benefit_amount: ₹1,000/month
eligibility: Widow aged 18 or above, resident of UP
Annual family income up to ₹2,00,000
Not receiving any other government pension
where_to_apply: sspy-up.gov.in (Integrated Pension Portal, UP)
common_rejection_reasons: Aadhaar not seeded to bank account

name: लक्ष्मीबाई सामाजिक सुरक्षा पेंशन / Bihar Laxmibai Samajik Suraksha Pension
authority: Social Welfare Department, Govt. of Bihar
benefit_amount: ₹400/month
eligibility: Widow aged 18–39 (40+ moves to IGNWPS with state top-up), resident of Bihar
Annual family income below ₹60,000
where_to_apply: serviceonline.bihar.gov.in (RTPS) or Block office

name: मुख्यमंत्री कल्याणी पेंशन योजना / MP Mukhyamantri Kalyani Pension
authority: Social Justice Department, Govt. of Madhya Pradesh
benefit_amount: ₹600/month
eligibility: Widow (kalyani) aged 18–79, resident of MP
Not an income-tax payee; not receiving another pension
where_to_apply: socialsecurity.mp.gov.in (Pension Portal MP)

name: मुख्यमंत्री एकल नारी सम्मान पेंशन / Rajasthan Ekal Nari Samman Pension
authority: Social Justice & Empowerment Department, Govt. of Rajasthan
benefit_amount: ₹1,150–₹1,500/month (slab by age)
eligibility: Widow/divorced/abandoned woman aged 18+, resident of Rajasthan
Annual family income up to ₹48,000
where_to_apply: ssp.rajasthan.gov.in or nearest e-Mitra kiosk

name: संजय गांधी निराधार अनुदान योजना / Maharashtra Sanjay Gandhi Niradhar Anudan Yojana
authority: Social Justice & Special Assistance Department, Govt. of Maharashtra
benefit_amount: ₹1,500/month (₹1,800 for families with more than one child)
eligibility: Destitute widow below 65, resident of Maharashtra
Annual family income up to ₹21,000 (rural)
where_to_apply: aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in or Tehsildar office

name: केंद्रीय/राज्य कर्मचारी पारिवारिक पेंशन / Family Pension for Government Employees (CCS Pension Rules)
authority: Department of Pension & Pensioners' Welfare (central) / State Finance Departments
benefit_amount: 30% of husband's last drawn pay (enhanced 50% for first 7-10 years), minimum ₹9,000/month + Dearness Relief
eligibility: Husband was a central or state government employee or pensioner
Payable to widow from the day after death, until remarriage
Applies whether he died in service or after retirement
where_to_apply: Husband's department office / pension-disbursing bank; status on bhavishya.nic.in for central employees
common_rejection_reasons: Family details not in service book

name: अनुकंपा नियुक्ति / Compassionate Appointment (Government Job for Family of Deceased Employee)
authority: Department of Personnel & Training (central) / State GAD rules
benefit_amount: Government job (Group C/D post) offered to widow or one dependent child
eligibility: Husband was a government employee who died in harness (while in service)
Family is in immediate financial distress
Application within the prescribed time limit (varies; typically within 5 years)
Subject to 5% ceiling of vacancies in the department
where_to_apply: Head of the department where the husband served (DoPT guidelines: dopt.gov.in)

name: ఎన్టీఆర్ భరోసా వితంతు పింఛను / Andhra Pradesh NTR Bharosa Widow Pension
authority: Society for Elimination of Rural Poverty (SERP), Govt. of Andhra Pradesh
benefit_amount: ₹4,000/month, delivered via Village/Ward Secretariat
eligibility: Widow aged 18 or above, resident of Andhra Pradesh
Monthly family income up to ₹10,000 (rural) / ₹12,000 (urban)
Not receiving another government pension
where_to_apply: Village/Ward Secretariat (free of cost) or sspensions.ap.gov.in

name: రైతు బీమా / Telangana Rythu Bima (Farmers' Group Life Insurance)
authority: Agriculture Department, Govt. of Telangana (with LIC)
benefit_amount: ₹5,00,000 one-time payout to nominee within ~10 days of farmer's death
eligibility: Deceased husband was a land-owning farmer aged 18–59 enrolled in Rythu Bima
Death from any cause is covered
Nominee (usually wife) receives payout directly to bank account
where_to_apply: Village Agriculture Extension Officer (AEO) / rythubandhu.telangana.gov.in

name: निर्माण श्रमिक मृत्यु लाभ / Construction Workers' Death Benefit & Family Pension (BOCW Welfare Boards)
authority: State Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Boards (under central BOCW Act 1996)
benefit_amount: ₹1–5 lakh ex-gratia on death (varies by state) + funeral assistance + family pension in several states
eligibility: Deceased husband was a registered construction worker (laborer) with the state BOCW board
Registration/membership active at the time of death
Widow or nominee applies to the same board
where_to_apply: State BOCW Welfare Board / labour department office or state labour portal (e.g. upbocw.in, tbocwwb.telangana.gov.in)
common_rejection_reasons: Worker not registered with the board
Claim filed outside the state of registration

name: विधवा वेतन / Karnataka Vidhava Vetana (Widow Pension)
authority: Directorate of Social Security & Pensions, Govt. of Karnataka
benefit_amount: ₹800/month
eligibility: Widow aged 18+, resident of Karnataka
Annual family income below ₹32,000
where_to_apply: sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in or Nadakacheri (Atalji Janasnehi Kendra)
